narrative.headline: 2018 Honda HR-V: electrical system tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 149 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 30%, followed by the fuel system and gasoline (both 18%). No fires or deaths are reported. The brakes draw far fewer complaints (10%) but the most crashes (5) and, along with the electrical system, the most injuries (2) of any area, so they are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 45 reports (30%), tied to 3 crashes and 2 injuries. Owners describe multiple dashboard warning lights coming on at once.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: 27 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Many reference a fuel system recall.

issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: 27 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Tied to the same fuel system recall.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Only 15 reports (10%) but the most serious by outcome: 5 crashes and 2 injuries. Test the brakes thoroughly.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 14 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Owners describe bucking or unexpected gear changes while driving.
